structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV Overcooling Risk
structured_payload_candidate.summary: CO2 DCV in cooling-only zones can cause overcooling because CO2 levels rise faster than cooling loads from occupants. Including heat in all zones with CO2 DCV is recommended.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: CO 2 DCV for cooling -only zones can lead to overcooling due to the faster rise in CO 2 levels from people in the room versus the increase in cooling loads from people. Including heat in all zones with CO 2 DCV is therefore recommended.

evidence_quote: CO 2 DCV for cooling -only zones can lead to overcooling due to the faster rise in CO 2 levels from people in the room versus the increase in cooling loads from people. Including heat in all zones with CO 2 DCV is therefore recommended.
evidence_text: CO 2 DCV for cooling -only zones can lead to overcooling due to the faster rise in CO 2 levels from people in the room versus the increase in cooling loads from people. Including heat in all zones with CO 2 DCV is therefore recommended.
judge_reason: Valid application guidance on CO2 DCV overcooling risk from Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: DCV Logic Not Provided for Cooling-Only Boxes
structured_payload_candidate.summary: DCV logic is not provided for cooling-only boxes because it would result in periods of overcooling due to the faster rise of CO2 from occupants compared to cooling load.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: DCV logic is not provided for cooling -only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO 2 levels due to  occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.

evidence_quote: DCV logic is not provided for cooling -only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO 2 levels due to  occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.
evidence_text: DCV logic is not provided for cooling -only boxes, because doing so results in periods of overcooling, as the CO 2 levels due to  occupants rises much faster than the cooling load due to occupants because of thermal mass.
judge_reason: Valid application guidance on DCV logic not provided for cooling-only boxes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Capability of Cooling-Only Terminal Units
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling-only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Cooling -only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.

evidence_quote: Cooling -only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.
evidence_text: Cooling -only terminal units can provide heating only when the AHU supply air temperature is more than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature.
judge_reason: Valid guidance on heating capability of cooling-only terminal units.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Reheat Box Recommended for High Minimum Ventilation or DCV
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling.

evidence_quote: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling.
evidence_text: If the minimum ventilation rate is more than 25% or so of the cooling maximum, or DCV is used, a reheat box is recommended to avoid overcooling.
judge_reason: Valid application guidance on reheat box recommendation for high minimum ventilation or DCV.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Maximum Endpoint per Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The cooling maximum endpoint is Vcool-max in Occupied, Cooldown, and Setup modes; and 0 in Warmup, Setback, and Unoccupied modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0

evidence_quote: Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
evidence_text: Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
judge_reason: Valid parameter specification for cooling maximum endpoint per zone group mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Maximum Endpoint per Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The heating maximum endpoint is Vheat-max in Occupied mode; 0 in Cooldown, Setup, Unoccupied; and Vcool-max in Warmup and Setback modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Heating maximum  Vheat -max  0 0 Vcool -max Vcool -max 0

evidence_quote: Heating maximum  Vheat -max  0 0 Vcool -max Vcool -max 0
evidence_text: Heating maximum  Vheat -max  0 0 Vcool -max Vcool -max 0
judge_reason: Valid parameter specification for heating maximum endpoint per zone group mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Minimum Endpoint per Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The minimum endpoint is Vmin* in Occupied mode; and 0 in Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, and Unoccupied modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0

evidence_quote: Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
evidence_text: Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
judge_reason: Valid parameter specification for minimum endpoint per zone group mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Alarm Threshold Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The constant value thresholds for airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms depend on the transducer and A/D converter accuracy; the value used should be the minimum accuracy of the combination.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The constant  value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

evidence_quote: The constant  value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
evidence_text: The constant  value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
judge_reason: Valid application guidance on airflow alarm threshold determination.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Testing/Commissioning Overrides
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide software switches interlocked to a system-level point to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, force to Vcool-max, force to Vmin, force damper full closed/open, and reset request-hours accumulator point to zero.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Provide software switches that interlock to a system-level point for the listed overrides.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to 
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  damper full closed/open , and
e. reset  request -hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).

evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to 
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  damper full closed/open , and
e. reset  request -hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).
evidence_text:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to 
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  damper full closed/open , and
e. reset  request -hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).
judge_reason: Valid commissioning step for testing/commissioning overrides.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm Level 3
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 3 alarm if measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 min while setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule for low airflow alarm level 3.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm Level 4
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 min while setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule for low airflow alarm level 4.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm Suppression for Importance Multiplier Zero
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for a zone that has an importance multiplier of 0 for its static pressure reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If a zone has an importance  multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: If a zone has an importance  multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: If a zone has an importance  multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule for low airflow alarm suppression.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 3 alarm if the fan serving the zone is OFF and the airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of Vcool-max or 50 cfm for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: fan OFF, airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 min
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule for airflow sensor calibration alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Damper Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if the damper position is 0% and the airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of Vcool-max or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: damper position 0%, airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 min while fan proven on
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minute s while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minute s while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minute s while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule for leaking damper alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling State Airflow Setpoint Limit when Supply Air Warmer than Room
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is cooling and supply air temperature from air handler > room temperature
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active airflow setpoint limited to no higher than minimum endpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

evidence_quote: If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .
evidence_text: If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .
judge_reason: Valid operational sequence for cooling setpoint limit when supply air warmer than room.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling State Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone state is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Cooling-loop output mapped to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint .

evidence_quote: When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint .
evidence_text: When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint .
judge_reason: Valid operational sequence for cooling state airflow setpoint mapping.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband State Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone state is deadband, the active airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the Zone State  is deadband , the active airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .

evidence_quote: When the Zone State  is deadband , the active airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
evidence_text: When the Zone State  is deadband , the active airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum endpoint .
judge_reason: Valid operational sequence for deadband state airflow setpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating State Airflow Setpoint Limit when Supply Air Not Warm Enough
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If the supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is heating and supply air temperature from air handler < room temperature + 3°C (5°F)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Active airflow setpoint limited to no higher than minimum endpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: If supply air temperature from the air handler is less than 3°C (5°F) above the room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Valid operational sequence for heating state airflow setpoint limit.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating State Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone state is heating, the heating loop output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the heating maximum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Heating loop output mapped to active airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the Zone State  is heating , the Heating Loop  output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from  the minim um endpoint  to the heating maximum endpoint .

evidence_quote: When the Zone State  is heating , the Heating Loop  output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from  the minim um endpoint  to the heating maximum endpoint .
evidence_text: When the Zone State  is heating , the Heating Loop  output shall be mapped to the active airflow setpoint  from  the minim um endpoint  to the heating maximum endpoint .
judge_reason: Valid operational sequence for heating state airflow setpoint mapping.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Damper Airflow Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate damper to maintain measured airflow at active setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint .

evidence_quote: The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint .
evidence_text: The VAV damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured airflow at the active setpoint .
judge_reason: Valid operational sequence for VAV damper airflow control.
